summary: Before we close, a look at some research that came across the desk.
Turning now to the new issues desk. Here is the prior day investment grade bond market report.

Stepping back to synthesize the picture, we are staring at a classic cross-asset divergence. Equities are eager to price in a Goldilocks scenario on the back of cheaper crude, but the rates market is refusing to let go of its hawkish posture.

Stepping back, we are watching a fascinating cross-asset divergence play out, continuing the theme from earlier this week where equities are eager to price in a Goldilocks scenario on cheaper crude, but the rates market is refusing to let go of its hawkish posture. This tension is particularly acute given the regime shift under way at the Fed.

Stepping back, today’s action continues the theme we have been tracking all week: a stark cross-asset divergence. Equities are eager to price in a Goldilocks scenario on the back of cheaper crude, but the rates market is refusing to let go of its hawkish posture. This tension is particularly acute given the regime shift under way at the Fed.

Stepping back, today’s action deepens the cross-asset divergence we have been tracking all week. This continues the theme from earlier this week where equities and credit want to believe in a supply-side miracle driven by cheaper crude, but the rates market has been forced to accept the reality of the Warsh Fed. This is a classic policy-regime tension.

Stepping back to synthesize the picture, today’s action deepens the stark cross-asset divergence we have been tracking all month. Equities and credit are desperately clinging to a goldilocks narrative, hoping that a supply-side miracle or cheaper domestic energy can bail them out. But the rates market is refusing to play along. This is a classic policy-regime tension.
